description: Creates a result file with the contents of the input but with a new name.  Where possible, this is done without copying the file but instead by simply making a link. author: David Eby
plan:
## RenameFile GenePattern Module — Implementation Plan

### 1. Overview
RenameFile is a GenePattern module that Creates a result file with the contents of the input but with a new name.  Where possible, this is done without copying the file but instead by simply making a link.  While it is possible to run this with a submitted file, the common use will be to change the name of a result file or a previously uploaded file.  Note that the original file will still be present in its original location.

### 2. Parameters
The module exposes 4 parameters:
- Required: input.file, output.filename, screen.filename, force.copy
- Optional: none

### 3. Command Line
```
<perl> <libdir>rename_file_for_GP.pl --inputFile <input.file> --outputFilename <output.filename> --screenFilename <screen.filename> --forceCopy <force.copy>
```

### 4. Docker Image
Docker image: 

### 5. Validation Strategy
- Validate all required parameters are provided
- Validate file formats match expected extensions
- Check parameter value ranges where applicable

### 6. Implementation Roadmap
Phase 1: Wrapper script implementation
Phase 2: Docker container setup
Phase 3: Parameter validation and error handling
Phase 4: Testing and documentation

parameters.name: output.filename
parameters.description: The name to give the output file

parameters.name: screen.filename
parameters.description: Automatically screen out problematic characters in the filename

parameters.name: force.copy
parameters.description: Force a copy to be made rather than a link.
